description:
Once a workflow is cancelled/reset by the daemon, the old workflow ID is gone with no audit trail. There is no way to see "TASK-108 has been dispatched and failed 50 times" or "TASK-108's last 3 workflows each failed in the requirements phase after 12 seconds." This makes debugging daemon issues nearly impossible.

## Fix
1. Add a `dispatch_history` array to the task JSON (or a separate `.ao/tasks/TASK-XXX.history.jsonl` sidecar file to avoid bloating the task object). Each entry:
   ```json
   {"workflow_id": "...", "started_at": "...", "ended_at": "...", "duration_secs": 42, "outcome": "failed", "failed_phase": "requirements", "failure_reason": "agent_exit_1"}
   ```
2. In `sync_task_status_for_workflow_result`, append to the history when a workflow reaches a terminal state (Completed, Failed, Cancelled).
3. Add `ao task history --id TASK-XXX` CLI command to display the dispatch history.
4. Expose via MCP as `ao.task.history` tool.
5. Include `total_dispatch_count`, `total_failures`, `avg_workflow_duration_secs` in `ao task get --json` output for quick triage.

## Key Files
- `crates/protocol/src/lib.rs` — Task type (add dispatch_history or sidecar path)
- `crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/runtime/runtime_daemon/daemon_task_dispatch.rs` — sync_task_status_for_workflow_result
- `crates/orchestrator-cli/src/cli_types/task_types.rs` — new TaskCommand::History variant
- `crates/orchestrator-cli/src/services/operations/ops_mcp.rs` — new ao.task.history MCP tool
title: Add per-task workflow execution history with failure tracking and timing
